request.description:
## Create Category

Creates a new task category.

---

### Method & URL

```
POST http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/categories

 ```

---

### Request Body Parameters

The request body should be sent as **JSON** (`application/json`).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `name` | string | ✅ Yes | The name of the category to create |

---

### Example Request Body

``` json
{
    "name": "Personal"
}

 ```

---

### Example Response

**Status:** `200 OK`

``` json
{
    "success": true,
    "data": {
        "id": 3,
        "name": "Personal",
        "created_at": "2026-07-01T04:07:19.000000Z",
        "updated_at": "2026-07-01T04:07:19.000000Z"
    }
}

 ```

## Get All Categories

Retrieves a list of all available categories in the system.

**Method:** `GET`  
**URL:** `http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/categories`

### Response

Returns a JSON object with a `success` flag and a `data` array containing all category records. Each category object includes the following fields:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | integer | Unique identifier for the category |
| `name` | string | Name of the category |
| `created_at` | string (ISO 8601) | Timestamp when the category was created |
| `updated_at` | string (ISO 8601) | Timestamp when the category was last updated |

### Example Response (200 OK)

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": [
    {
      "id": 2,
      "name": "Work",
      "created_at": "2026-07-01T04:07:01.000000Z",
      "updated_at": "2026-07-01T04:07:01.000000Z"
    },
    {
      "id": 3,
      "name": "Personal",
      "created_at": "2026-07-01T04:07:19.000000Z",
      "updated_at": "2026-07-01T04:07:19.000000Z"
    }
  ]
}

 ```

## Get Category by ID

Retrieves the details of a single category using its unique identifier.

### Method
`GET`

### URL
`http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/categories/:id`

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type    | Required | Description                          |
|-----------|---------|----------|--------------------------------------|
| `id`      | integer | Yes      | The unique ID of the category to retrieve. |

### Request Headers

No additional headers required.

### Request Body

None.

### Response

**Success (200 OK)**

Returns a JSON object containing the category details.

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "id": 3,
    "name": "Personal",
    "created_at": "2026-07-01T04:07:19.000000Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-07-01T04:07:19.000000Z"
  }
}
```

| Field        | Type    | Description                              |
|--------------|---------|------------------------------------------|
| `success`    | boolean | Indicates whether the request succeeded. |
| `data.id`    | integer | The unique ID of the category.           |
| `data.name`  | string  | The name of the category.                |
| `data.created_at` | string | ISO 8601 timestamp of when the category was created. |
| `data.updated_at` | string | ISO 8601 timestamp of the last update.  |

### Notes
- Replace the `3` in the URL with the actual category ID you want to retrieve.
- Returns a `404` or error response if the category with the given ID does not exist.

Updates an existing category by its ID.

**Method:** PUT  
**URL:** `http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/categories/:id`

### Path Parameters
| Parameter | Type    | Required | Description                        |
|-----------|---------|----------|------------------------------------|
| id        | integer | Yes      | The unique identifier of the category to update |

### Request Body
Send a JSON object with the fields you want to update.

| Field | Type   | Required | Description              |
|-------|--------|----------|--------------------------|
| name  | string | Yes      | The new name of the category |

**Example Request Body:**
```json
{
    "name": "Study"
}
```

### Response
Returns a success flag and the updated category object.

**Example Response (200 OK):**
```json
{
    "success": true,
    "data": {
        "id": 3,
        "name": "Study",
        "created_at": "2026-07-01T04:07:19.000000Z",
        "updated_at": "2026-07-01T04:08:35.000000Z"
    }
}
```

Creates a new task in the system.

## Endpoint

`POST /api/tasks`

## Request Body (JSON)

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `category_id` | integer | The ID of the category this task belongs to |
| `title` | string | The title/name of the task |
| `description` | string | A detailed description of the task |
| `completed` | boolean | The completion status of the task (`false` = not completed, `true` = completed) |

## Response

Returns a JSON object with the following structure:

- **`success`** _(boolean)_: Indicates whether the request was successful.
    
- **`data`** _(object)_: The newly created task object, containing:
    - `id` — Unique identifier of the created task
        
    - `category_id` — The category the task is assigned to
        
    - `title` — The task title
        
    - `description` — The task description
        
    - `completed` — Completion status
        
    - `created_at` — Timestamp of when the task was created
        
    - `updated_at` — Timestamp of the last update
        

## Example Response

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "id": 4,
    "category_id": 2,
    "title": "Study API",
    "description": "Read documentation",
    "completed": false,
    "created_at": "2026-07-01T04:18:15.000000Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-07-01T04:18:15.000000Z"
  }
}

 ```

## Get All Tasks

Retrieves a list of all tasks stored in the system.

**Method:** `GET`  
**URL:** `http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/tasks`

---

### Response Structure

Returns a JSON object with the following fields:

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `success` | `boolean` | Indicates whether the request was successful |
| `data` | `array` | Array of task objects |

#### Task Object

Each item in the `data` array contains:

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `id` | `integer` | Unique identifier of the task |
| `title` | `string` | Title of the task |
| `category_id` | `integer` | ID of the category this task belongs to |
| `description` | `string` | Detailed description of the task |
| `completed` | `integer` | Completion status — `0` for incomplete, `1` for complete |
| `created_at` | `string` | ISO 8601 timestamp of when the task was created |
| `updated_at` | `string` | ISO 8601 timestamp of when the task was last updated |
| `category` | `object` | Nested category object associated with the task |

#### Nested `category` Object

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `id` | `integer` | Unique identifier of the category |
| `name` | `string` | Name of the category |
| `created_at` | `string` | ISO 8601 timestamp of when the category was created |
| `updated_at` | `string` | ISO 8601 timestamp of when the category was last updated |

---

### Example Response

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": [
    {
      "id": 2,
      "title": "Finish Laravel",
      "category_id": 2,
      "description": "Complete CRUD",
      "completed": 0,
      "created_at": "2026-07-01T04:17:23.000000Z",
      "updated_at": "2026-07-01T04:17:23.000000Z",
      "category": {
        "id": 2,
        "name": "Work",
        "created_at": "2026-07-01T04:07:01.000000Z",
        "updated_at": "2026-07-01T04:07:01.000000Z"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

## Get Task by ID

Retrieves the details of a single task identified by its unique ID.

**Method:** `GET`
**URL:** `http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/tasks/{id}`

---

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type    | Required | Description                        |
|-----------|---------|----------|------------------------------------|
| `id`      | integer | Yes      | The unique identifier of the task. |

---

### Response Structure

A successful request returns a JSON object with the following structure:

| Field                     | Type    | Description                                          |
|---------------------------|---------|------------------------------------------------------|
| `success`                 | boolean | Indicates whether the request was successful.        |
| `data`                    | object  | The task object.                                     |
| `data.id`                 | integer | Unique identifier of the task.                       |
| `data.title`              | string  | Title of the task.                                   |
| `data.category_id`        | integer | ID of the category this task belongs to.             |
| `data.description`        | string  | Detailed description of the task.                    |
| `data.completed`          | integer | Completion status (`0` = incomplete, `1` = complete).|
| `data.created_at`         | string  | ISO 8601 timestamp of when the task was created.     |
| `data.updated_at`         | string  | ISO 8601 timestamp of when the task was last updated.|
| `data.category`           | object  | The category associated with the task.               |
| `data.category.id`        | integer | Unique identifier of the category.                   |
| `data.category.name`      | string  | Name of the category.                                |
| `data.category.created_at`| string  | ISO 8601 timestamp of when the category was created. |
| `data.category.updated_at`| string  | ISO 8601 timestamp of when the category was updated. |

---

### Example Response

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "id": 2,
    "title": "Finish Laravel",
    "category_id": 2,
    "description": "Complete CRUD",
    "completed": 0,
    "created_at": "2026-07-01T04:17:23.000000Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-07-01T04:17:23.000000Z",
    "category": {
      "id": 2,
      "name": "Work",
      "created_at": "2026-07-01T04:07:01.000000Z",
      "updated_at": "2026-07-01T04:07:01.000000Z"
    }
  }
}
```

## Update Task

Updates an existing task identified by its ID. Use this endpoint to modify any of the task's fields, such as its title, description, category, or completion status.

**Endpoint:** `PUT /api/tasks/{id}`

---

### Request Body

Send a JSON object with the fields you want to update:

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `category_id` | integer | The ID of the category this task belongs to. |
| `title` | string | The title or name of the task. |
| `description` | string | A brief description of the task. |
| `completed` | boolean | Indicates whether the task has been completed (`true`) or not (`false`). |

**Example:**
```json
{
  "category_id": 2,
  "title": "Finish Laravel Project",
  "description": "CRUD Completed",
  "completed": true
}
```

---

### Response

Returns a JSON object with a `success` flag and the updated task data.

**Example Response (200 OK):**
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "id": 2,
    "title": "Finish Laravel Project",
    "category_id": 2,
    "description": "CRUD Completed",
    "completed": true,
    "created_at": "2026-07-01T04:17:23.000000Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-07-01T04:20:07.000000Z"
  }
}
```

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `success` | boolean | `true` if the update was successful. |
| `data.id` | integer | The unique identifier of the task. |
| `data.title` | string | The updated title of the task. |
| `data.category_id` | integer | The category the task belongs to. |
| `data.description` | string | The updated description of the task. |
| `data.completed` | boolean | The updated completion status of the task. |
| `data.created_at` | string (ISO 8601) | Timestamp when the task was originally created. |
| `data.updated_at` | string (ISO 8601) | Timestamp when the task was last updated. |

Deletes a specific task by its ID.

**Method:** `DELETE`
**URL:** `http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/tasks/:id`

Replace `:id` in the URL with the numeric ID of the task you want to delete (e.g., `/api/tasks/2`).

**Response:**
On success, the server returns a JSON object confirming the deletion:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Task deleted successfully"
}
```

> **Note:** This action is irreversible. Once a task is deleted, it cannot be recovered.

## Delete Category

Deletes a specific category by its ID.

**Method:** `DELETE`  
**URL:** `http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/categories/{id}`

The category ID is passed as a path segment in the URL (e.g., `/api/categories/3` deletes the category with ID `3`).

### Successful Response (200 OK)

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Category deleted successfully"
}
```

A `success: true` value confirms the category was found and permanently removed.
